name: Plant Defense Response Process
description: Plant Defense Response Process in Plants. This process represents a higher-level biological mechanism distinct from molecular/biochemical processes covered in GLMP.
mermaid:
graph TD
    A25[Pathogen Recognition] --> B25[Receptor Activation]
    B25 --> C25[Signal Transduction]
    C25 --> D25[Oxidative Burst]
    D25 --> E25[Cell Death]
    C25 --> F25[Gene Expression]
    F25 --> G25[Defense Compound Synthesis]
    G25 --> H25[Systemic Acquired Resistance]
    H25 --> I25[Enhanced Protection]
    
    style A25 fill:#ff6b6b,color:#fff
    style B25 fill:#ffd43b,color:#000
    style C25 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style D25 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style E25 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style F25 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style G25 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style H25 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style I25 fill:#b197fc,color:#fff

sources.title: Programming Framework: A Universal Process Visualization Methodology
sources.notes: Higher-level biological process visualization: Plant Defense Response Process. Created using the Programming Framework methodology for organismal and ecological process analysis.

sources.title: Plant innate immunity
sources.authors: Jones, JD., Vance, RE., Dangl, JL.

sources.title: Plant Plasma Membrane Receptors: From Perception to Signal Transduction
A Renaissance of Elicitors: Perception of Microbe-Associated Molecular Patterns and Danger Signals by Plants

notes: Higher-level biological process. GLMP covers biochemical/molecular processes; this database covers organismal/ecological processes.
